title: glycosma bolanderi
intro: Glycosma bolanderi, also known as Bolander's glycosma, is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae, and its bark, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Glycosma bolanderi include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and skin conditions like eczema and acne.
